scooter_rental.notes: Most popular way to get around; be cautious on mountain roads

bicycle_rental.notes: Good for flat areas; Ubud has hills

taxi_ride_share.notes: Always use app-based taxis, avoid unmarked cabs

private_driver.notes: Best for day trips to temples; driver waits while you visit

motorbike_rental.notes: For experienced riders only — more power for mountain roads

subway_bus.notes: Excellent coverage; IC card (Suica/Icoca) works on all trains and buses

bicycle_rental.notes: City is very bike-friendly; e-bike recommended for Arashiyama hills

taxi.notes: Expensive but comfortable; required for late-night trips

walking.notes: Higashiyama and Gion are best explored on foot

metro.notes: T-Casual 10-trip card is best value at $12

bicycle_rental.notes: Bicing city bikes available with app; city has great bike lanes

walking.notes: Gothic Quarter, Eixample, and waterfront all walkable

bts_skytrain.notes: Best way to avoid Bangkok traffic; covers main tourist areas

tuk_tuk.notes: Negotiate price BEFORE getting in; mostly for short hops or tourists

taxi_ride_share.notes: Grab is the safest and most transparent option

scooter_rental.notes: Dangerous in Bangkok traffic — only for experienced riders

boat.notes: Chao Phraya Express boat is fast and cheap along the river

rental_car.notes: Essential for seeing Iceland properly; 4WD recommended for Highlands

bicycle_rental.notes: Good for city center in summer; too cold and icy in winter

bus.notes: Strætó app covers city buses; limited coverage outside Reykjavik

organized_tour.notes: Golden Circle, Northern Lights, and South Coast tours widely available from Reykjavik

bicycle_rental.notes: The Amsterdam way — dedicated bike lanes everywhere, flat city

tram_metro.notes: GVB app or I Amsterdam card; trams go everywhere in center

canal_boat.notes: Electric canal boat rental is magical — no license needed up to 15hp

walking.notes: City center is very walkable; canal ring is best on foot

mrt_subway.notes: World-class MRT covers entire city; EZ-Link card works everywhere

bus.notes: Google Maps bus directions are perfect in Singapore

grab.notes: Cheaper than taxis; Grab dominates Singapore ride-sharing

walking.notes: Central areas are connected by air-conditioned covered walkways

tram_metro.notes: Viva Viagem card; Tram 28 for sightseeing (but crowded)

bicycle_rental.notes: Lisbon is hilly — e-bike strongly recommended

scooter_rental.notes: Lime/Circ electric scooters everywhere; no license needed

taxi_uber.notes: Bolt (like Uber) is cheapest ride-share in Lisbon

metro.notes: Gold and Silver class; covers main tourist sites including Dubai Mall and airport

taxi.notes: Dubai Taxi is reliable; Careem app also excellent

rental_car.notes: Good for desert excursions; parking free at malls

water_taxi.notes: Traditional abra (wooden boat) crosses Dubai Creek for 1 AED — unmissable

metro.notes: Most efficient; Paris Visite pass for 2-5 days; zone 1-3 covers all sights

bicycle_rental.notes: Vélib city bikes available with app; flat along the Seine

taxi_uber.notes: G7 or Uber are reliable; taxis from stands don't use apps

walking.notes: Left Bank and Marais neighborhoods are best on foot

tram_metro.notes: Excellent public transport; 24hr pass for CZK 120 ($5) covers everything

bicycle_rental.notes: Czech capital is very bike-friendly with riverside paths

taxi_bolt.notes: Bolt is cheapest; avoid taxi ranks (tourist pricing)

walking.notes: Old Town, Malá Strana, and Vinohrady all walkable; cobblestones — wear good shoes

metro.notes: Only 2 lines (A and B); Termini, Colosseum, and Spanish Steps are on it

taxi.notes: Only use white official taxis from official stands

scooter_rental.notes: Electric moped (Cooltra app) available; authentic Roman experience

walking.notes: Historic center is 2.5km across — most sights are walkable in a day

subway.notes: Best way to get around; 24/7 service; OMNY tap to pay with any contactless card

bicycle_rental.notes: Citi Bike available everywhere in Manhattan and Brooklyn

taxi_uber.notes: Uber/Lyft; yellow cabs in Manhattan; surge pricing in evenings

walking.notes: Manhattan is a grid — easy to navigate; walkable blocks

jr_pass_subway.notes: Suica IC card is essential; IC card works on all trains, buses, and at convenience stores

bicycle_rental.notes: Good in flat areas; many wards have Docomo bike-share stations

taxi.notes: Expensive but immaculately clean and reliable; drivers often don't speak English

rental_car.notes: Best for visiting Chichen Itza and Tulum; highway tolls ~$10 round trip

bus_ado.notes: ADO buses to Chichen Itza, Playa del Carmen, Tulum are comfortable and cheap

colectivos.notes: Shared mini-vans along Hwy 307 — cheapest way to move between Cancun and Tulum

taxi.notes: Fixed-rate taxis; always agree price before getting in

speedboat.notes: Main transfer between Male and local islands (20-90 min)

seaplane.notes: Required for remote atolls; spectacular views

dhoni_boat.notes: Traditional wooden boat; best for exploring nearby islands

snorkel_equipment.notes: Snorkeling gear rental from most resorts and local islands

metro.notes: Covers Copacabana, Ipanema, and city center; avoid rush hour

cable_car.notes: Sugarloaf cable car and Corcovado cog train are attractions in themselves

taxi_uber.notes: Uber is recommended over street taxis for safety

bicycle_rental.notes: Excellent bike path along Ipanema and Copacabana beachfronts
